items.question: What does inertia (WCSS) measure in k-means?
options: Total squared distance from points to their assigned centroids
Mean silhouette across all clusters
Chance-corrected agreement with ground-truth labels
Number of support vectors per cluster
items.answer: Total squared distance from points to their assigned centroids
items.explanation: Inertia is the within-cluster sum of squares—the objective k-means minimizes.

items.question: Silhouette score near +1 for a point generally indicates:
options: The point is closer to its own cluster than to the nearest other cluster
The point is equidistant from all clusters
The point should be removed as an outlier
Ground-truth labels match cluster IDs perfectly
items.answer: The point is closer to its own cluster than to the nearest other cluster
items.explanation: High s(i) means b(i) >> a(i)—good cohesion and separation.

items.question: Why is the elbow method used with inertia plots?
options: Inertia always increases with k
Inertia decreases with k but marginal gains shrink— the bend suggests a reasonable k
Inertia is undefined for k > 10
Elbow plots replace the need for any other metric
items.answer: Inertia decreases with k but marginal gains shrink— the bend suggests a reasonable k
items.explanation: The bend is heuristic; combine with silhouette and domain needs.

items.question: Adjusted Rand Index (ARI) requires:
options: Only feature matrix X
Cluster labels and ground-truth reference labels
A trained classifier with probability outputs
Labeled data for training but not for evaluation
items.answer: Cluster labels and ground-truth reference labels
items.explanation: ARI is an external metric comparing two partitions.

items.question: Which statement about silhouette and inertia is correct?
options: They always peak at the same k
Inertia favors larger k monotonically; silhouette can peak at moderate k
Silhouette requires ground-truth labels
Inertia is bounded between -1 and 1
items.answer: Inertia favors larger k monotonically; silhouette can peak at moderate k
items.explanation: Use multiple diagnostics—neither alone is sufficient.
